summary: Applied ADR 0052 live by adding Loki to the monitoring plane and converging Alloy-based host and guest log shipping, while also completing the live rollout of the corrected ADR 0053 tracing stack on monitoring-lv3.

verification.observed: grafana-server, influxdb, loki, alloy, tempo, otelcol-contrib, and lv3-prometheus were all active on monitoring-lv3, with listeners confirmed on 3100, 3200, 3201, 9090, 14317, and 14318.
Grafana returned the managed Loki datasource as {name:"Loki Logs", type:"loki", url:"http://127.0.0.1:3100"}.
The Loki host label values were ["backup-lv3","docker-build-lv3","docker-runtime-lv3","monitoring-lv3","nginx-lv3","postgres-lv3","proxmox_florin"].

Recent Loki series for host=docker-runtime-lv3 and source=docker included control-plane containers such as windmill-windmill_server-1, netbox-netbox-worker-1, windmill-windmill_worker-1, windmill-windmill_worker_native-1, and netbox-redis-1.
The Loki series query for host=nginx-lv3, service=nginx, log_type=access returned 4 active streams.
After emitting a verification-only logger event on proxmox_florin, Loki query_range returned the adr0052-verification syslog identifier from the host journal stream.
Tempo search returned the service.name value ["mail-gateway"] from the live trace store.
Prometheus query for traces_service_graph_request_total returned a live series with client=user, server=mail-gateway, connection_type=virtual_node, cluster=lv3, and value 83 at verification time.

notes: The deterministic Proxmox host verification used a single logger event tagged adr0052-verification and should be treated as verification-only operational noise.
The final mainline converge also moved Tempo onto its managed gRPC port 3201, which avoids the Loki default listener collision and lets the monitoring stack rerun cleanly from main.
